NOTICE OF CONTINUED HEARING RE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION RE WORK TO LEGALIZE THE GARAGEAT 2255-57 PINE STREET, SAN FRANCISCO FILED BY DEFENDANT BOLEX LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: DEFENDANT BOLEX LLC'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION RE WORK TO LEGALIZE THE GARAGE AT 225-57 PINE STREET SAN FRANCISCO CALIFORNIA - THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ORDERED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER IN ORDER TO RESOLVE THEIR ISSUES. AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE HEARING, THE PARTIES REACHED A STIPULATION AS STATED ON THE RECORD. THE PARTIES WERE VOIE DIRED ON THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATION. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATION AS ITS ORDER ENFORCEABLE UNDER CCP 664.6. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT BOLEX LLC'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN CASE FOR LIMITED PURPOSE OF SEALING COURT RECORDS; MOTION TO SEAL COURT RECORDS IS DENIED. COURT RECORDS ARE PRESUMED TO BE OPEN UNLESS THEY ARE REQUIRED BY LAW TO BE CONFIDENTIAL. THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY SEALING OF THE COMPLAINT, THE CROSS COMPLAINT, ALL RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS, THE MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION, THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION, AND THIS MOTION. MOVING PARTY HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THERE EISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THESE RECORDS, THAT THIS OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD, THAT A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THESE RECORDS ARE NOT SEALED, THAT THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. SEE GENERALLY CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 2.550(B)(1) AND 2.550(D). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 618

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. JOINDER OF PLAINTIFFS ERIC EDELSON AND ERIKA EDELSON'S AND NON-OPPOS, PURSUANT TO SETTLEMENT TO BOLEX, LLC'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN CASE FOR LIMITED PURPOSES OF SEALING COURY RECORDS; MOTION TO SEAL COURT RECORDS IS DENIED. COURT RECORDS ARE PRESUMED TO BE OPEN UNLESS THEY ARE REQUIRED BY LAW TO BE CONFIDENTIAL. THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY SEALING OF THE COMPLAINT, THE CROSS COMPLAINT, ALL RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS, THE MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION, THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT AND OPPOSITION, AND THIS MOTION. MOVING PARTY HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THESE RECORDS, THAT THIS OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD, THAT A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THESE RECORDS ARE NOT SEALED, THAT THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. SEE GENERALLY CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 2.550(B)(1) AND 2.550(D).JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
(Joinder) And Non-Oppos, Pursuant To Settlement To Bolex, Llc'S Motion To Re-Open Case For Limited Purposes Of Sealing Courr Records; Motion To Seal Court Records
Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK JOHN RICE OF MCNEAL SILVERSON & RICE, TEL. NO. 472-3434 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. JOINDER OF PLAINTIFFS ERIC EDELSON and ERIKA EDELSON's And Non-Oppos, Pursuant To Settlement To Bolex, Llc'S Motion To Re-Open Case For Limited Purposes Of Sealing Coury Records; Motion To Seal Court Records is Denied. Court records are presumed to be open unless they are required by law to be confidential. The moving party has failed to provide facts sufficient to justify sealing of the Complaint, the Cross Complaint, all responsive pleadings, the motion to expunge lis pendens and all papers filed in support and opposition, the motion for preliminary injunction and all papers filed in support and opposition, and this motion. Moving party has not established that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to these records, that this overriding interest supports sealing the record, that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if these records are not sealed, that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. See generally California Rules of Court 2.550(b)(1) and 2.550(d).Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm

Court records are presumed to be open unless they are required by law to be confidential. The moving party has failed to provide facts sufficient to justify sealing of the Complaint, the Cross Complaint, all responsive pleadings, the motion to expunge lis pendens and all papers filed in support and opposition, the motion for preliminary injunction and all papers filed in support and opposition, and this motion. Moving party has not established that there eists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to these records, that this overriding interest supports sealing the record, that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if these records are not sealed, that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Off Calendar Aug-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark J. Rice, Esq. of McNeil Silveira Rice & Wiley, Tel. 415.472.3434, for Defendant and moving party;
Daron D. Tong, Esq. of Miller & Tong, Tel. 415.409.5222, for Plaintiff and opposing party Eric Edelson, also present in court.

RULING: DEFENDANT BOLEX LLC's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens, or in the alternative, For Order Requiring An Undertaking; Request For Judicial Notice - The matter was argued. The court ordered the parties to meet and confer in order to resolve their issues. At the conclusion of the hearing, the parties reached a stipulation as stated on the record. The parties were voie dired on the terms of the stipulation. The court adopted the terms of the stipulation as its order enforceable under CCP 664.6. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
